title: Woodland Wins Houston Open
excerpt: Gary Woodland takes the Texas Children's Houston Open with a stunning -18 score, Nicolai Højgaard comes in second at -17
body:
Texas Children's Houston Open Recap

It was a thrilling weekend at the Texas Children's Houston Open, with Gary Woodland emerging victorious with a score of -18. The American golfer played consistently well throughout the tournament, with rounds of 64, 63, and 65.

Leaderboard Highlights

Nicolai Højgaard came in second at -17, while Min Woo Lee and Michael Thorbjornsen tied for third at -12. Other notable performances included Sam Stevens and Jason Day, who both finished at -11.

Woodland's Score
-18
Højgaard's Score
-17
Lowest Round
63

What's Next

The golfing world now turns its attention to the DP World Tour, with the Hero Indian Open scheduled to take place from March 26-29. The LIV Golf tour is also set to continue, with its next event taking place in Singapore. Meanwhile, the PGA Tour will head to the Masters in April, one of the most highly anticipated events in the golfing calendar.

The Texas Children's Houston Open was an incredible tournament, with some truly impressive golf on display. We can't wait to see what the rest of the season has in store.
